profile.name: Philip Stiber
profile.headline: Cybersecurity Leadership | IT Infrastructure Management | M&A Technology Integration
profile.hook: Two companies become one — and the technology has to merge overnight: secure, audited, and running by Monday. That's my job.
profile.summary: For 15 years I've worked at the intersection of three roles most people pick only one of: the engineer who builds the systems, the security leader who defends and governs them, and the integrator who fuses entire companies together after an acquisition. I came up hands-on — Active Directory, networks, virtualization, PowerShell — and never traded that depth away as I moved into leadership. Today I run global infrastructure and M&A integration at Tilray Brands, where I folded 10 acquisitions into one secure platform in 12 months, migrated 900+ employees across Microsoft Entra tenants, and built the SOX ITGC, system-hardening, and incident-response controls that keep auditors and executives confident. CISSP- and CISM-certified, I translate fluently between the server room and the boardroom: I can architect the network, write the script, pass the audit, and brief the C-suite on the risk — often in the same week.
profile.tagline: Engineer × Security Leader × M&A Integrator × Hands-on through Executive-facing experiences × Identity, Cloud, Networking, GRC

crossing.title: Build it, then defend it
crossing.body: Most security leaders have never run the systems they are securing. I architect the Active Directory, network, and virtualization estate and then harden it — segmentation, next-gen firewalls, application whitelisting, CIS controls — so the defense is designed by someone who knows exactly how the machine works underneath.

crossing.title: Merge companies, not just mailboxes
crossing.body: M&A integration is where identity, cloud, networking, and governance collide at once. I collapse acquired tenants, domains, and email into a single governed Entra / Microsoft 365 estate — on a 60-to-90-day clock — without dropping security posture or breaking the business mid-flight.

crossing.title: Make compliance operational
crossing.body: GRC only delivers when it lives in daily operations. I turn SOX ITGC, NIST 800-53, and CIS into working controls — SLAs, business impact analyses, patch cycles, access reviews — instead of audit-season slideware that nobody runs.

crossing.title: Lead without losing the keyboard
crossing.body: I lead distributed teams of network engineers, cloud architects, and admins while staying technical enough to do the work myself. That earns trust in both directions: engineers respect it, executives rely on it.

crossing.title: Resilience as a habit
crossing.body: From a 48-hour COVID-19 remote-work cutover for 160 staff to DR-ready virtualization, business continuity is baked into how I build — not bolted on after the incident.

rareStats.label: CISSP + CISM held together
rareStats.note: Most security professionals carry one or the other. Both at once validates that I can build and defend the systems (CISSP) and govern the program and its risk (CISM). Fewer than 5% of security practitioners hold both.

rareStats.label: of M&A deals miss their goals
rareStats.note: Harvard Business Review and others have long reported most mergers fail to deliver their expected value — with technology integration repeatedly named a leading cause. I have personally delivered 10 integrations in 12 months.

rareStats.label: deep, not just broad
rareStats.note: Candidates are usually deep-technical OR strategic. The overlap of hands-on infrastructure, security leadership, and M&A delivery is a narrow slice of the talent pool.

rareStats.label: hands-on through executive-facing
rareStats.note: Technical depth I never gave up — still writing the PowerShell and reading the KQL while owning the strategy and the audit.

rareStats.label: CISSP + CISM + active builder
rareStats.note: Holding both premier certs while actively shipping infrastructure as code, detection engineering, and automation puts this profile in a vanishingly small group: the practitioner who can also own the boardroom conversation.

education.program: Coursework in Network Engineering & Windows Server Administration

github.intro: Public work that backs up the resume — secure-by-design infrastructure as code, detection engineering, and automation. The same disciplines, shipped in the open.

repos.name: Blueteamthreathunting

repos.why: Proactive hunting for the identity attacks that drive the majority of breaches.

repos.name: Sentineladvisor

repos.why: Turns a raw SIEM into well-tuned, actionable detections instead of alert noise.

repos.name: Merakicorrelator

repos.why: Bridges network operations and security analytics — hands-on with the Meraki estate I ran at enterprise scale.

repos.name: TeraformMultiregionAD

repos.why: Resilient, DR-ready identity infrastructure as code — the continuity mindset, automated.

repos.name: CiscoMerakiSDWanAutoVPNAzureGuidance

repos.why: Hybrid network architecture connecting branch sites to cloud — the exact pattern behind multi-site enterprises.

repos.why: Pipeline automation and secure software delivery — DevSecOps fundamentals.

repos.name: ClaudeDevSecOpseagents

repos.name: claudecheatsheets

repos.name: tokenwatcher

repos.why: Range and a builder's curiosity — a native C# app, shipped for fun.

experience.summary: Leads global infrastructure and high-tempo M&A integration for a multinational beverage and cannabis enterprise.

brief.headline: Integrated 10 acquisitions within 12 months — fueling Tilray Beverages' rise to the #4 craft brewer in the U.S.
brief.situation: A multinational beverage and cannabis enterprise on an aggressive acquisition spree, inheriting a sprawl of incompatible networks, identities, and email systems across 65+ sites in North America and Europe.
brief.action: Built and led a 10-person infrastructure team running back-to-back M&A takeovers — AB InBev and Molson Coors plant cutovers, 6 Entra tenant-to-tenant migrations, and AD / Meraki / O365 consolidations — while wiring SOX ITGC, system hardening, and incident response into daily operations.
brief.outcome: 10 organizations integrated within 12 months · 900+ employees onboarded to unified governed identity · 4 production facilities transferred with zero unplanned downtime — accelerating the synergy capture behind the division's climb to #4 in the U.S. craft market.

achievements.text: Orchestrated high-priority IT M&A across 10 acquired organizations in 12 months, directly contributing to Tilray Beverages becoming the 4th-largest craft brewer in the U.S.
achievements.impact: Accelerated value capture from acquisitions: each integration delivered on schedule meant faster revenue consolidation and synergy realization, directly advancing the growth strategy that vaulted the beverage division to #4 in the U.S. market.

achievements.text: Coordinated IT acquisition events with AB InBev — full system and network takeovers of 4 production facilities in 90 days.
achievements.impact: Protected production continuity through the ownership transfer: zero unplanned downtime across four revenue-generating plants meant no lost output or missed shipments during handover, de-risking the deal's operational P&L.

achievements.text: Directed post-acquisition transitions with Molson Coors — 6 major migrations in 60 days (Meraki org transfers, G-Suite→O365, Exchange→O365, Active Directory takeovers).
achievements.impact: Compressed the integration timeline so the business could exit costly Transition Service Agreements early — every retired legacy contract cut recurring spend and removed a dependency on the seller.

achievements.text: Completed 6 Microsoft Entra tenant-to-tenant migrations, onboarding 900+ employees and thousands of digital assets within a year.
achievements.impact: Unified identity gave 900+ employees governed, single sign-on access from day one — speeding workforce productivity, enabling consistent MFA and policy enforcement, and eliminating duplicate licensing across fragmented tenants.

achievements.text: Led a distributed team of 10 (network engineers, cloud architects, sysadmins) supporting 15+ production facilities, 20 brew pubs, and 30 offices across North America and Europe.
achievements.impact: Sustained always-on operations across 65+ sites on two continents with a lean team — high engineer-to-site leverage held infrastructure overhead down while protecting the uptime revenue depends on.

achievements.text: Aligned infrastructure operations with cybersecurity pillars: SLA reviews, BIAs, system hardening, SOX ITGC, and incident management/response.
achievements.impact: Made IT an audit-ready, board-defensible function: SOX ITGC compliance and documented business impact analyses reduced regulatory and financial-reporting risk for a publicly traded company and protected market trust.

experience.summary: Matured the security program of a professional services firm and rebuilt its core identity and network estate.

brief.headline: Moved 160 staff fully remote in 48 hours and rebuilt the firm's identity, network, and security estate.
brief.situation: A professional-services firm running aging, end-of-life infrastructure with limited security maturity — then forced into an overnight remote-work pivot by the COVID-19 pandemic.
brief.action: Matured the security program to CIS benchmarks (segmentation, application whitelisting, firm-wide Palo Alto NGFW, cloud IAM), stood up proactive security operations (threat hunting, incident response, patch cycles), and architected a greenfield Active Directory + O365 environment — co-authoring the COVID-19 technical strategy with the COO.
brief.outcome: 160 employees productive remotely within 48 hours · 300 endpoints and 50 servers reimaged across three 48-hour windows · a measurably hardened posture that protected client confidentiality and uninterrupted billing.

achievements.text: Established CIS framework maturity via security improvement plans: application whitelisting, network segmentation, next-gen firewalls, and cloud IAM.
achievements.impact: Raised measurable security maturity to protect the firm's most valuable asset — client confidentiality — lowering breach risk, strengthening cyber-insurance posture, and reinforcing the trust that wins and keeps clients.

achievements.text: Oversaw enterprise-wide Palo Alto NGFW deployment across all firm locations, replacing end-of-life equipment and increasing network visibility.
achievements.impact: Closed an end-of-life risk and audit/insurance gap across every office while gaining network-wide visibility — reducing the likelihood of a costly outage or breach the firm could not afford.

achievements.text: Built core security processes: phish-alert reporting, incident management, threat hunting, patching cycles, and coordinated incident response.
achievements.impact: Shifted the firm from reactive to proactive defense: faster detection and response shrink attacker dwell time and the financial and reputational cost of an incident — critical for a custodian of sensitive financial data.

achievements.text: Served as M&A technology advisor for the Collins Barrow Kawarthas & Durham consolidation, leading IT due diligence.
achievements.impact: Gave leadership a clear view of technology risk and integration cost before signing — informing deal valuation and enabling a smoother, lower-cost post-merger combination.

achievements.text: Designed, built, and deployed a new Active Directory + O365 environment enabling full remote connectivity in under 10 months; reimaged 300 endpoints and 50 servers across three 48-hour maintenance windows.
achievements.impact: Delivered a modern, remote-capable platform that unlocked workforce flexibility and future growth — executed inside tight maintenance windows to avoid disrupting billable client work.

achievements.text: Transitioned 160 employees to remote work in 48 hours by co-authoring the firm's COVID-19 technical strategy with the COO.
achievements.impact: Kept the firm fully operational and billing through a crisis while competitors stalled — business continuity that protected revenue and client commitments during a black-swan event.

experience.summary: Modernized core systems and prepared the firm's IT estate for merger due diligence.

brief.headline: Modernized core systems and made the IT estate merger-ready, supporting a clean firm consolidation.
brief.situation: An accounting firm running aging on-prem infrastructure and preparing for a merger that demanded full transparency into its technology and costs.
brief.action: Virtualized the entire physical server fleet to Hyper-V 2016, migrated email from Exchange 2010 to Office 365, standardized GPO and Windows hardening baselines, and assembled the asset inventory and cost models for due diligence.
brief.outcome: A modern, DR-ready, fully documented environment with lower hardware cost and improved uptime — plus the transparent asset and cost data that de-risked the merger and supported a fair valuation.

achievements.text: Built IT asset inventory, process documentation, and cost models to satisfy merger due-diligence requirements.
achievements.impact: Produced the transparency a merger requires: accurate asset and cost data supported fair valuation and de-risked the transaction for both parties.

achievements.text: Migrated email from Exchange 2010 to Office 365, reducing exposure and improving reliability.
achievements.impact: Cut the cost and risk of aging on-prem mail while improving uptime — modernizing a mission-critical communication channel the business relies on every day.

achievements.text: Virtualized all on-prem physical servers to Hyper-V 2016, improving scalability and DR readiness.
achievements.impact: Improved disaster-recovery readiness and hardware efficiency — lowering capital cost and shrinking the recovery time that protects the business after a failure.

achievements.text: Standardized GPO baselines and implemented Windows 10 / Server 2016 hardening guidelines.
achievements.impact: Made security and onboarding repeatable: consistent baselines reduce configuration drift, speed new-hire productivity, and shrink the attack surface as the firm scales.

experience.summary: Delivered end-to-end IT modernization for legal firms, CPA practices, and small businesses across the eastern GTA.

brief.headline: Brought enterprise-grade reliability and security to dozens of small firms across the eastern GTA.
brief.situation: Legal, CPA, and small-business clients needing modernization but lacking in-house IT depth or enterprise budgets.
brief.action: Executed complex AD and Exchange migrations, server virtualization, and Office 365 deployments — each right-sized to the client and backed by ongoing support and seamless cutovers.
brief.outcome: Enterprise-grade reliability and security at small-business cost, with no lost productivity through change — converting technical upgrades into durable client trust and referrals.

achievements.text: Performed complex AD domain and Exchange migrations, server virtualization, and Office 365 deployments.
achievements.impact: Modernized client environments so small firms could operate with enterprise-grade reliability and security — turning technical upgrades into client business resilience.

achievements.text: Managed migration programs and ongoing support, sustaining seamless operations post-cutover.
achievements.impact: Protected client operations through and after change: seamless cutovers meant no lost productivity, preserving the trust that retains clients and drives referrals.

achievements.text: Delivered tailored, fit-for-purpose systems deployments aligned to each client's requirements.
achievements.impact: Matched spend to need — right-sized solutions avoided over-investment and delivered measurable ROI for cost-conscious small businesses.

experience.summary: Supported major migrations and line-of-business continuity for legal and CPA clients.

brief.headline: Kept 30+ legal line-of-business apps and their users running through major platform migrations.
brief.situation: Legal and CPA practices dependent on specialized line-of-business software, facing risky platform transitions across Exchange, BlackBerry BES, Active Directory, and Windows 7/8.
brief.action: Acted as a fractional IT department — running migrations and new-client onboarding, managing MSP helpdesk escalations, and supporting 30+ specialized applications.
brief.outcome: Continuity of the 30+ applications the practices bill against, with minimal disruption to billable work — protecting client deadlines and active case work through technically risky change.

achievements.text: Supported major migrations — Exchange, BlackBerry BES, Active Directory, and Windows 7/8 deployments.
achievements.impact: Kept executives and staff productive through platform change — minimizing disruption to billable work during technically risky transitions.

achievements.text: Delivered IT consulting, project management, and systems administration for legal firms, CPA practices, and small businesses.
achievements.impact: Acted as a fractional IT department, letting professional firms focus on their core revenue work while technology stayed dependable and secure.

achievements.text: Assisted MSP helpdesk escalation management and maintained support for 30+ legal line-of-business applications.
achievements.impact: Sustained continuity of the 30+ specialized applications law practices bill against — fast escalation resolution protected client deadlines and active case work.
